stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed for autonomous scientific discovery.
It posits that the primary bottleneck in achieving autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed for autonomous scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck for AI-driven science is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that surpass human-designed methods.

stories.title: German court ruling declares Google's AI Overviews are Google's own words and makes it liable for false answers
stories.author: the-decoder.com via gspr
bullet_points: A German court ruled that Google's AI Overviews are considered Google's own generated content.
The ruling establishes legal liability for Google regarding false or inaccurate answers provided by its AI.
This decision signals growing regulatory scrutiny over the accuracy and authorship of generative AI outputs.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in its MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The tool aims to bridge communication platforms with local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Amazon’s data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water last year
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Amazon disclosed that its global data centers consumed 2.5 billion gallons of water last year.
The disclosure comes amid growing scrutiny over water and energy usage in new AI data center construction.
The report is notable as it follows a one-year data center moratorium enacted in Seattle, which some Amazon employees supported.

stories.title: "This cannot continue": Xbox leaders lay out "hard truths" behind sagging brand
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Xbox leadership has acknowledged significant internal issues contributing to the brand's decline.
The article suggests a 'crisis' within the Microsoft gaming division based on internal assessments.
The piece offers a critical look at the strategic challenges facing Xbox in the current market.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 introduces a new pipeline to address the gap in scientific knowledge orchestration for LLM-based research agents.
The system moves beyond simplistic methods (like abstracts and citation counts) to capture deep scientific elements such as entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.
This work aims to enable more robust scientific reasoning within AI agents by converting raw documents into structured, agent-native knowledge.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived by composing a stated decomposition of the same query, leveraging operad theory.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) for evaluating LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion over model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
The research introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent (with tools/filesystem) rather than just a model call.
This concept is being practically implemented in production coding agents, notably in Anthropic's dynamic workflows.

stories.title: Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
stories.author: rarisma
bullet_points: Anthropic issued an apology regarding issues with the 'Claude Fable' guardrails.
The core issue involves 'invisible distillation' guardrails, suggesting a failure in model safety mechanisms.
The story highlights ongoing challenges in ensuring transparency and reliability in advanced AI models.

stories.title: I Lead AI Agents Every Day - Here Are 5 Shifts No Standard Tells You How to Make
stories.author: itskondrat
bullet_points: Google DeepMind is investing $10M into multi-agent safety research.
The article discusses five non-standard shifts required for leading AI agents.
The topic directly addresses critical safety concerns in advanced AI systems.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Siri won’t be your AI girlfriend
stories.author: Thomas Ricker
bullet_points: Apple's new Siri is being designed to avoid the sycophantic behavior seen in competitor chatbots (OpenAI, Google).
Apple leadership, via Craig Federighi, confirmed that Siri has built-in mechanisms to know when to remain silent.
The announcement addresses a key design philosophy difference between Apple's AI and other major AI platforms.

stories.title: The bill that would let Jimmy Kimmel sue Brendan Carr is here
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: A new bipartisan bill, the JAWBONE Act, would allow Americans to sue if a government official illegally coerces a tech company.
The law targets illegal coercion against social media, AI, or broadcasting companies regarding content removal.
The bill was introduced by Senators Ted Cruz (R-TX) and Ron Wyden (D-OR) in the Senate Commerce Committee.

stories.title: A warrantless wiretap law is about to expire — but surveillance networks aren’t actually ‘going dark’
stories.author: Gaby Del Valle
bullet_points: Section 702 of FISA, which permits warrantless wiretapping, is facing expiration.
Congress failed to pass a three-week extension, leading to a potential lapse in the surveillance program.
The article suggests that despite the lapse, surveillance networks are unlikely to completely cease operations.

stories.title: After nearly breaking, NASA's Deep Space Network "worked well" on Artemis II
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's Deep Space Network (DSN) performed effectively during the Artemis II mission, despite initial concerns about its operational status.
The report suggests that the actual operational capacity of the DSN exceeded the documented or expected levels.
The finding highlights the real-world performance capabilities of critical space infrastructure.

stories.title: Alaskans will be flying blind after NSF decommissions ocean monitoring network
stories.author: Paula Dobbyn, Inside Climate News
bullet_points: The National Science Foundation (NSF) is decommissioning an ocean monitoring network.
This action poses risks to Alaska's substantial fishing industry.
Coastal communities in Alaska are also threatened by the loss of the monitoring data.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail when reasoning requires different strategies for similar problems.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Mana is a new sim-to-real framework designed to address the complex challenge of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools.
The research tackles the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and handling contact-rich interactions in robotics.
It aims to advance the field beyond rigid object manipulation to cover the more complex domain of functional grasping with tools.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It investigates how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The research focuses on improving open-ended spatial reasoning capabilities in AI agents.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences.
This approach aims to address the resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale nature of traditional reproducibility checks.
The study tested the LLM's performance by comparing its analysis against original findings across 76 published studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's cognitive impact.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical perspective on AI's influence that other models miss.
A central concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing how AI systems affect human cognition.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for Slovak.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It proposes using automata-theoretic machinery (like product game construction) to derive structural insights about adversarial networks.
The core argument is that the focus should shift from runtime constraints to pre-deployment defensibility analysis.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics involving articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces Mana, a general sim-to-real framework designed to tackle this complexity.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping policies for physically complex tools.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the low-resource Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage compared to existing multilingual benchmarks for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles behind knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
A new metric, Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), is introduced to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study validates its findings using Split CIFAR-100 and a sequentially trained ResNet-18 across ten tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model for predicting spatially varying wildfire spread.
Develops an optimization framework to design effective, targeted aerial suppression strategies.
Addresses operational and environmental uncertainty in wildfire management using gradient-based design.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that automata-theoretic machinery (like specification compilation and product game construction) provides structural insights into adversarial networks.
The core contribution is shifting the focus from enforcing runtime constraints to performing defensibility analysis of AI systems.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation of current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continually update their knowledge and behavior in response to progressive environmental changes.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning tasks.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail when reasoning requires different solution strategies for similar problems.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution to improve reasoning.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training data samples influence a model's output (e.g., identifying sources of toxic behavior in LLMs).
Influcoder distills gradient influence rankings from decoders to achieve this data attribution.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents.
Proposes 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface.
Aims to reduce context consumption by abstracting low-level, step-wise tool calls from the main reasoning trace.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method suggests that an LLM's direct answer to a complex query should match the answer derived from a predefined decomposition of that same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency and semantic entropy) used for evaluating LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the low-resource Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage compared to existing multilingual benchmarks for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Operads for compositional reasoning in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: The paper proposes using 'operads,' a mathematical structure, to provide a rigorous foundation for question decomposition in LLMs.
Question decomposition (breaking complex queries into sub-queries) is a known technique to improve LLM reasoning but currently lacks a formal mathematical basis.
The defined 'questions operad' ($Q$) models the many-in, one-out operations inherent in composing answers from sub-queries.

stories.title: Why New Language Features Need to Target AI Agents, Not Developers
stories.author: moderncpp
bullet_points: The core argument is that programming language design should shift focus from human developers to AI agents.
The premise is that AI agents are increasingly capable of writing production-ready code.
This necessitates optimizing language features for machine consumption rather than solely for human readability/convenience.
